verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: While on the one hand it is a joy to us to learn that our brethren are solicitous about their children in fatherly charity, on the other we count it no less a matter for sadness when neither regard for other brethren nor consideration of their priestly office avails to restrain them from unlawful doings.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: How serious, then, and how harsh is the complaint against our brother Paulinus, bishop of the city of Tegessis, made by his clerics and by those who are in sacred orders, I have no doubt is well known to thy Fraternity, since what has reached us from a distance cannot have been hidden from thee who art near at hand.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, since there is need of great caution lest this bodily injury which they complain of at his hands in excess of his powers should be ventured on with allowance, or should grow worse by being connived at, manifest excesses should ever be so suppressed by canonical control that one proceeding may serve as a reproof of what is past and a rule for the future.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Accordingly it becomes thee, together with our most beloved common brother the bishop Columbus, and with other priests whom you may think fit to call on, to sift the case between our above-named brother and his clergy by means of a thorough investigation.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, if the complaint of the petitioners stands with truth, so correct ye this thing by a regular reformation, that he may both be made aware what evil thing he has done and learn for the future not to exceed the limits of his office.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And suffer him not, as is said to be the case, to disregard the rank of thy position, lest his contempt be to his risk and to thy blame.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For whatever is committed by an inferior, unless it be carefully corrected, reflects on the person who occupies the superior place.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: That other matter also, namely that the same our brother Paulinus is said to confer ecclesiastical orders for money, you should fully and very strictly enquire into.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, if it should clearly appear to be so, as we hope will not be the case, let your zeal for God so kindle itself to avenge this wrong that both the avarice of the ordainer may be turned into a penalty, and, the unlawful ordination being void of effect, the person ordained may not enjoy the longed-for object of his ambition.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Herein we exhort you and before all things admonish you, that your Fraternity study to be so solicitous that, before the iniquity of simoniacal heresy shall gain strength in your parts from the offence of one, it may be cut off from the root by the pruning-hook of your sentence after a council diligently held.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For whosoever does not, in consideration of his office, burn vehemently to correct this atrocity, let him not doubt that he will have his portion with him from whom this peculiar enormity took its beginning.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And so, as we have said, you must act vigilantly and earnestly, that your council, which up to this time, under God’s keeping, has been preserved from any bad repute of this kind, may not by any possibility be polluted and ruined by the poison of this wickedness.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Furthermore, we have given orders to Hilarus our Chartularius, that, if the case should require it, he defer not to join you.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore, should it be necessary, inform him by your letters of the need of his coming to you, to the end that you, together with him, may be able, God helping you, to determine all these things in a salutary way.
